notes: Constructors reject every absent required root field while optional fields retain explicit presence.
The more specific ECMA-262 recommendation is selected over PCRE and bounded by a match timeout.
Schema patterns use the pinned regexp2 ECMAScript mode rather than Go RE2 syntax.
Extension names require the exact x- prefix.
The feature set is explicitly 1.4.
All canonical patch releases in the supported 1.4 feature line use identical semantics.
Every non-negative canonical 1.4 patch is accepted without distinct semantics.
This governs future specification authors; the implementation explicitly rejects un-inventoried future feature lines.
This hypothetical future-release guarantee cannot govern current 1.4 tooling before that specification exists.
The module has no implicit file-loading or naming surface.
The optional CLI and default-path behavior are intentionally absent from the core module.
Canonical documents emit the specification's exact camel-case standard field names.
Duplicate JSON object member names are rejected.
All member names are unique before patterned fields are interpreted.
The module preserves rich text but exposes no renderer to which this conditional requirement applies.
Markdown rendering and security feature selection remain caller-owned.
The transport-neutral discovery adapter exposes only the mandated rpc.discover name.
This governs the upstream specification's example publication; the module pins and tests those examples without relabeling their older feature lines.
The exact authoritative schema and its companion are pinned and checksum-verified offline.
Every inline and reusable Schema Object is compiled as Draft 7 without lossy numeric conversion.
Specification extensions are retained as exact arbitrary JSON fields on extensible objects.
Contact email values are checked with a syntax-aware address parser.
Contact URL values must be absolute URI references.
A descriptor cannot be constructed without an object or boolean schema.
The descriptor deprecation advisory is preserved explicitly and defaults to false only when absent.
By-name parameter assignment retains each descriptor name as the JSON-RPC parameter key.
Error codes use canonical arbitrary-precision integer lexemes.
Predefined protocol errors are assumed and produced by the caller-owned JSON-RPC runtime, not duplicated in document methods.
Clearly multiline, long, or multi-sentence error messages produce a non-fatal advisory.
Every inline External Documentation Object URL must be absolute.
Optional API metadata is preserved for presentation and other caller-owned uses.
Terms-of-service values must be absolute URI references.
License URL values must be absolute URI references.
Link method names must resolve to exactly one inline or referenced method.
Method identity is checked after bounded reference resolution.
Method deprecation is exposed as an explicit advisory value and defaults to false only when absent.
Custom application errors are represented as an optional owned method collection.
Error-code uniqueness is checked after bounded reference resolution.
Method names are unique across inline and referenced methods.
Parameter names are unique across inline and referenced descriptors.
Required ordering is enforced after descriptor references are resolved.
An explicit result is represented only by the Content Descriptor or Reference union.
Result absence is preserved and exposed as notification-only method semantics.
Server rich text is preserved verbatim for caller-owned GitHub Flavored Markdown rendering.
Relative server URL templates are accepted and preserved.
Server variables use their required defaults unless a validated override is supplied.
Every extensible model embeds the same validated specification-extension field contract.
Only canonical semantic versions in the supported line are accepted.
Parsing interprets the openrpc value through the supported feature-line contract.
